expectations.text: The blog post is between 500 and 700 words
expectations.evidence: Blog word count is ~587 words (counted from 'The program was eight weeks old...' to '[Book a call at apexstrengthcoaching.com]')

expectations.text: The blog post opens with a specific scene, moment, or claim — not a definition or backstory paragraph
expectations.evidence: Opens mid-scene: 'The program was eight weeks old and Marcus had skipped four of the last seven sessions.' — drops reader into a specific moment with a named person.

expectations.text: The blog post contains zero em dashes (—)
expectations.evidence: No em dashes found in the blog post. All connective punctuation uses commas or periods.

expectations.text: The Instagram caption is between 130 and 200 words (not counting hashtags)
expectations.evidence: Caption is ~172 words (not counting the hashtag line). Within the 130-200 range.

expectations.text: The Instagram caption includes at least 8 hashtags
A LinkedIn post is present and distinct from the Instagram caption
expectations.evidence: LinkedIn post is present (section 2B) and takes a thought-leadership angle with full sentences, professional framing, and a different structure from the Instagram caption.

expectations.text: The carousel section includes exactly 8 slides with headings and sub-text
expectations.evidence: 8 slides present: Slide 1 (Hook) through Slide 8 (Close + CTA). Each has a Heading and Sub-text field.

expectations.text: The reel concept includes at least 6 scenes with time codes
The output does not use the phrase 'transform your body' or 'melt fat'
expectations.evidence: Neither phrase appears anywhere in the content package.

expectations.text: The writing reflects Apex's direct, practical voice — not generic motivational language
expectations.evidence: Blog uses evidence-backed language ('Neuromuscular coordination, tissue remodeling...'). No motivational poster phrases. IG caption ends with 'something about the approach needs to change, not you' — direct and specific.
